title: polyscias collina
intro: Polyscias collina, also known as the Polyscias or Mascagnia, is a plant species in the Araliaceae family that is native to tropical regions and is widely cultivated for its medicinal and ornamental value. The plant is valued for its leaves, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of Polyscias collina have been found to have antioxidant, anti-diabetic, and anti-cancer properties, providing numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Polyscias collina are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
